morphology.summary: A compact semantic-control capsule for liability; morphology is not the focus of this seed entry.

semantic_drift.summary: Semantic-control usage focuses on present operational meaning rather than historical drift for liability.

present_usage.summary: Legal or practical responsibility for an obligation, loss, or fault.

senses.definition: Legal or practical responsibility for an obligation, loss, or fault.

examples.text: The word liability changes what claims the agent may safely make.

cultural_frame.summary: liability appears in communication where tone, risk, evidence, or commitment must be controlled.

frames.label: semantic control
frames.description: Frames liability as a term whose pragmatic force can change the safe output path.

pragmatics.summary: Liability language can sound like legal admission, financial exposure, or formal responsibility.

stances.label: control-sensitive

expansion.paragraph: liability is represented here as a semantic-control term: its present usage and pragmatic force help decide whether an agent should ask, check evidence, avoid overclaiming, or revise before export.
